Container.Name: UmlautAdaptarrEX
Container.Overview:
UmlautAdaptarrEX 2.0 - Umlaut/German-title proxy for Sonarr, Radarr, Lidarr, Readarr and Prowlarr. Complete Next.js + Fastify + Prisma rewrite of the legacy .NET UmlautAdaptarr.
The proxy sits between your *Arr stack and your indexers, normalizing German titles (umlauts, alternative spellings) so releases that would otherwise be missed get matched correctly. Includes a built-in Web UI (port 5007) for setup, monitoring and live log streaming. SQLite is used by default - no external database required, the database file is persisted in /data which maps to your appdata share.
Three ports are exposed: 5005 (Fastify API + WebSocket logs - this is the URL you point your *Arrs at), 5006 (TCP indexer proxy for Prowlarr's HTTP-CONNECT tunneling) and 5007 (Web UI). On first start the app boots into a setup wizard at http://[IP]:5007/setup where you configure your *Arrs, Prowlarr and the indexer-proxy credentials.
Container.Description:
UmlautAdaptarrEX 2.0 - Umlaut/German-title proxy for Sonarr, Radarr, Lidarr, Readarr and Prowlarr. Complete Next.js + Fastify + Prisma rewrite of the legacy .NET UmlautAdaptarr.
The proxy sits between your *Arr stack and your indexers, normalizing German titles (umlauts, alternative spellings) so releases that would otherwise be missed get matched correctly. Includes a built-in Web UI (port 5007) for setup, monitoring and live log streaming. SQLite is used by default - no external database required, the database file is persisted in /data which maps to your appdata share.
Three ports are exposed: 5005 (Fastify API + WebSocket logs - this is the URL you point your *Arrs at), 5006 (TCP indexer proxy for Prowlarr's HTTP-CONNECT tunneling) and 5007 (Web UI). On first start the app boots into a setup wizard at http://[IP]:5007/setup where you configure your *Arrs, Prowlarr and the indexer-proxy credentials.
The container fixes ownership of the appdata directory to PUID:PGID (default 99:100, the Unraid `nobody:users` pair) on every start, so no manual `chown` is required.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: HTTP port for the Next.js web UI. Open this in your browser to reach the setup wizard and admin panel.

Config.Name: API Port
Config.Description: Fastify API + WebSocket port. This is the URL you point your *Arr instances at (e.g. http://unraid-ip:5005/<apikey>/sonarr/api). Also serves /api/admin, /api/auth, /titlelookup and /ws/logs.

Config.Name: Indexer Proxy Port
Config.Description: TCP HTTP-CONNECT proxy for Prowlarr indexers. Configure this as Prowlarr's HTTP proxy so indexer responses get rewritten on the fly.

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the SQLite database (umlautadaptarrex.db) and the title-cache. Do NOT delete - it contains all your *Arr connections, API keys and matching state. Ownership is fixed automatically on container start (see PUID/PGID).

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: UID the app runs as. Default 99 = Unraid `nobody`, which matches the rest of your appdata shares. The entrypoint chowns /data to PUID:PGID on every start.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: GID the app runs as. Default 100 = Unraid `users` group.

Config.Name: TZ
Config.Description: IANA timezone (e.g. Europe/Berlin, UTC, America/New_York). Affects timestamps in logs and the Web UI.

Config.Name: LOG_LEVEL
Config.Description: Pino log level: trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal.
